candidates.direction_name: Retime the PTX wait-group and CTA barrier seam on the current 128x128 anchor

score_breakdown.reason: Best remaining bounded exploit on the current PTX anchor after re-anchor is already complete.

ranking_notes: This is the highest-ranked open family in the current frontier.
Barrier is no longer theoretical; it regressed versus the previous run while the rest of the profile stayed mostly flat.
The change is still local to the active PTX microkernel and does not require another restore step.
candidates.hypothesis: The latest run is already sitting on the best measured current-workload PTX surface, but the active microkernel still spends 5.93% of active warp issue slots on barrier stalls while active warps remain stuck at 16.64%. The next bounded gain is to retime the `cp.async.wait_group` plus `__syncthreads()` seam inside the 128x128 PTX microkernel so the current two-stage pipeline hands off more cleanly without growing shared memory or reintroducing the higher-register control path.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue layered on top of an occupancy_latency_hiding_issue in the active 128x128 PTX hot-band kernel
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if reducing barrier stall requires extra shared-memory stages, pushes registers back above the current 198/thread level, or fails to beat the 46.532095 ms current-workload anchor.

target_hotspots.explanation: The active hot-band PTX kernel is still register-limited at 198 registers per thread.
Achieved occupancy remains pinned near the theoretical floor, so barrier work must not hurt active warps.

expected_local_changes: Reschedule the `cp_async_wait_group_0()` and `__syncthreads()` placement inside the steady-state loop.
Keep the two-stage shared-memory footprint unchanged.
Preserve the current grouped-row mapping and PTX accumulator/export path while only tightening the handoff seam.

guardrail_metrics.reason: Barrier tuning is not worth taking if latency-hiding gets worse.
The local anchor already restored the lower-register surface; barrier retiming must not give that back.
A better handoff must not simply trade barrier time for worse scoreboard stalls.

candidates.direction_name: Trim PTX export-address and store-path scalar live state after the MMA loop

score_breakdown.reason: Low-scope scalar cleanup remains credible even though source correlation fell back to section-level evidence.

ranking_notes: This stays below the barrier retime because the latest structured delta explicitly regressed barrier while register pressure only improved slightly.
The win condition is small but auditable: lower registers or flatter live-state pressure without sacrificing tensor activity.
This direction stays within the current winning geometry and does not depend on historical restores.
candidates.hypothesis: The primary bottleneck is still occupancy and latency hiding, and source-level export rows are unavailable, so the most defensible low-scope secondary move is to cut scalar/live-state pressure in the PTX writer path. The 128x128 PTX microkernel still computes row/column-derived store pointers and funnels through per-warp shared export helpers after the MMA loop; trimming that address math and tightening the store helper interface can pay down register pressure without touching the main tile geometry.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue with tail_overhead_or_generic_path_issue concentrated in the PTX export/store path
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the export/store-path cleanup does not improve registers or active warps, or if it destabilizes the PTX writer correctness path.

target_hotspots.explanation: Register pressure remains the top structured hotspot in the absence of usable source correlation.
Scalar cleanup only matters if it lets the same geometry feed the tensor path more consistently.

expected_local_changes: Narrow store-path temporaries in the PTX microkernel epilogue.
Push row/column-to-pointer conversion as late as possible into the final writer helpers.
Keep shared export scratch size and launch geometry unchanged.

guardrail_metrics.reason: Scalar cleanup should help feed the tensor path, not dilute it.
Do not trade scalar cleanup for a worse synchronization seam.

candidates.direction_name: Transplant the lower-register half-panel budget into the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot

score_breakdown.reason: High-upside orthogonal branch, but it is not the cheapest next budget unit while the active PTX path still has local seams.

ranking_notes: This remains alive because it is the best non-PTX-local branch against the same register-pressure evidence.
It drops to third because the implementation surface is wider and recent rounds show the workload is sensitive to restores and path flips.
Use it if the first two bounded current-anchor exploits both flatten out.
candidates.hypothesis: If the current 128x128 PTX path cannot move off the occupancy floor with local control-path cleanup, the next orthogonal branch should be the 256x128 pivot, but only with the lower-register half-panel budget transplanted into the correctness-safe writer path. That is the only geometry jump still consistent with the latest NCU evidence because it explicitly attacks the same register-limited occupancy problem instead of chasing stale accepted-surface history.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue addressed by a geometry and register-budget change rather than another PTX-local retime
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the pivot transplant broadens the implementation beyond the hot-band path, fails correctness, or does not materially improve registers/active warps.

target_hotspots.explanation: Any geometry jump must buy back register budget, not just reshuffle the same pressure.

expected_local_changes: Reuse the correctness-safe 256x128 writer path instead of reviving the stale historical restore surface.
Transplant only the lower-register half-panel staging and accumulator ownership choices.
Keep the change limited to the hot-band branch and its launch selection.

guardrail_metrics.reason: This branch has a much larger correctness surface than the local PTX exploits.
The whole point of the transplant is to recover occupancy headroom.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
